name: Christmas Carols
description: Silent Night, Hark!, O Holy Night, Jingle Bells — the songs of the season and how they came to be.

questions.q: In what year was the carol 'Silent Night' first performed?

source.notes: Performed Christmas Eve 1818 in the Nikolauskirche, Oberndorf, Austria.

questions.q: What is the original language of 'Silent Night'?
What is the Latin title of the carol known in English as 'O Come, All Ye Faithful'?
Which Victorian carol about a 10th-century king tells of a charitable act on the Feast of Stephen (Dec 26)?
What 1857 song, originally written for Thanksgiving, is now considered a Christmas standard?
choices: We Wish You a Merry Christmas

source.notes: Written by James Lord Pierpont as 'The One Horse Open Sleigh.'

questions.q: Which 1843 Charles Dickens novella popularized phrases like 'Bah! Humbug!' and 'God bless us, every one'?

source.notes: Featuring Ebenezer Scrooge, Bob Cratchit, Tiny Tim, Jacob Marley, and the three ghosts.

questions.q: Who composed the music for 'Hark! The Herald Angels Sing' (adapted from a cantata)?
Which Christmas carol's lyrics include 'O night divine, the night when Christ was born'?

source.notes: French original: 'Cantique de Noël', composed in 1847 by Adolphe Adam.

questions.q: Which century saw the earliest documented Christmas hymns?

source.notes: Early Latin hymns like 'Veni redemptor gentium' by Ambrose.

questions.q: On what date in 1914 did German and British soldiers reportedly sing carols together in the WWI 'Christmas Truce'?

source.notes: Truces broke out across the Western Front; 'Stille Nacht' is often cited as a song shared across the lines.

questions.q: Which Hawaiian-themed Christmas song was a Bing Crosby and Andrews Sisters hit in 1949?
Whose 1958 hit 'Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree' was recorded when she was just 13?

source.notes: Lee was just shy of 14 when she recorded it.

questions.q: Which Mariah Carey song is now the highest-streamed Christmas song each year?
choices: All I Want for Christmas Is You

source.notes: Released 1994; first reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2019.

questions.q: Which Wham! Christmas song was famously kept off the UK #1 in 1984 by Band Aid's 'Do They Know It's Christmas?'
choices: Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go

source.notes: 'Last Christmas' finally reached UK #1 in 2021, 36 years after release.

questions.q: What is the title of the famous Christmas anti-hunger fundraiser song co-written by Bob Geldof and Midge Ure in 1984?

source.notes: Recorded by Band Aid for Ethiopian famine relief.

questions.q: What is the original meaning of the word 'carol' (before its Christmas association)?

source.notes: From Old French 'carole', a ring dance with singing.

questions.q: Which English Cathedral popularized the 'Festival of Nine Lessons and Carols' on Christmas Eve since 1918?

source.notes: Broadcast every Christmas Eve since 1928 by the BBC.

questions.q: What number of items in total does a person receive in 'The Twelve Days of Christmas' by Day 12 (cumulative)?
What does the 'noel' in many Christmas carols mean?
Which song repeats the line 'How great our joy!' as part of its German-derived chorus?
